heading: Administration, protection, and development
label: 410yyy–2 Administration, protection, and development
text: The administration, protection, and development of the Ocmulgee National Monument shall be under the supervision of the Secretary of the Interior subject to the provisions of the Act entitled “An Act to establish a National Park Service, and for other purposes”, approved , as amended.12See Change of Name note below.1See References in Text note below.2August 25, 1916
notes: References in TextThe Act entitled “An Act to establish a National Park Service, and for other purposes”, approved , referred to in text, is , , known as the National Park Service Organic Act, which enacted sections 1, 2, 3, and 4 of this title and provisions set out as a note under , National Park Service and Related Programs. Sections 1 to 4 of the Act were repealed and restated as , Crimes and Criminal Procedure, and section 100101(a), chapter 1003, and sections 100751(a), 100752, 100753, and 102101 of Title 54 by , §§ 3, 4(a)(1), 7, , , 3260, 3272. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Tables. For disposition of former sections of this title, see Disposition Table preceding .August 25, 1916Dec. 19, 2014act Aug. 25, 1916, ch. 40839 Stat. 535section 100101 of Title 54section 1865(a) of Title 18Pub. L. 113–287128 Stat. 3094section 100101 of Title 54
CodificationSection was formerly classified to .section 447c of this title

Change of NameOcmulgee National Monument was renamed Ocmulgee Mounds National Historical Park by , , , which is classified to .Pub. L. 116–9, title II, § 2102(b)(1)133 Stat. 724section 410yyy–3(b)(1) of this titleMar. 12, 2019
